sources.label: Markets Daily - Toromont sets new 12-month high (June 4, 2026)
Toromont Investor Relations - Latest News (no new releases since Jun 6)
Investing.com - Toromont Q1 2026 earnings call transcript (beat, AVL data-center enclosure contribution)

verdicts.rankRationale: No new releases on the IR wire since June 6; today's -0.58% is noise and yesterday's hold already priced the +32% YTD run. Mild negative drift only: the stock printed a fresh 52-week high June 4 and now trades essentially at the ~C$227.56 consensus target, so residual latent-alpha on this strict screen erodes slightly without new AI-chain evidence.
verdicts.bull: AVL data-center enclosure business (majority stake, Jan 2026) added $129M revenue and $0.19 EPS in Q1 and is expanding production, giving Toromont a real, growing direct line into compute-campus buildouts beyond the generic Cat-dealer thesis.
verdicts.neutral: Q1 beat (EPS C$1.14 vs C$1.09e) is six weeks old and already reflected in the rerating; with shares at the consensus target and a 0.56/share dividend just gone ex, the setup is fairly priced pending the July 28 Q2 print.
verdicts.bear: +32% YTD into a 52-week high with no new contract or capacity news since June 6 is classic post-rerating chase; on a strict latent-nodes screen the name is no longer hidden, and a Canadian construction/mining slowdown would hit the core dealer business regardless of AVL.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 earnings on July 28 showing AVL data-center enclosure backlog/revenue acceleration or a named hyperscaler campus win.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Multiple compression as a now-discovered, consensus-target-priced industrial if AI-campus construction equipment demand fails to show up in orders.
